description: Each (ticker, date) pair must be unique to satisfy the database UNIQUE constraint and prevent double-counting.
Stock prices must be strictly positive; zero/negative prices indicate data corruption.
Rows missing any required price/volume/date are dropped.
Within a trading day, open and close must lie within [low, high] and low <= high; any violation is impossible and indicates corruption.
All 8 required columns (date, OHLC, adj_close, volume, ticker) must be present; raises ValueError if not.
Zero-volume days can indicate trading halts or data gaps; flagged for review but not dropped.
Single-day moves above 50% almost always indicate an unadjusted split or data error rather than a real return.
Calendar gaps > 5 days within a ticker may indicate missing trading-day records (excludes normal weekend/holiday gaps).
